---
id: BudgetCreated
name: Budget Created
version: 0.0.1
schemaPath: schema.json
summary: A new budget was created.
---

<NodeGraph />

## Example payload

```json title="Example Payload"
{
  "event_id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000",
  "timestamp": "2024-07-04T14:48:00Z"
}
```

## Schema (JSON)

<SchemaViewer file="schema.json" search="true" expand="true" />


 ## Raw Schema:schema.json

{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "event_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uuid",
      "description": "Unique identifier for the event"
    },
    "timestamp": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time",
      "description": "Timestamp of the event"
    }
  },
  "required": ["event_id", "timestamp"]
}

---
id: BudgetDeleted
name: Budget Deleted
version: 0.0.1
schemaPath: schema.json
summary: A budget was deleted.
---

```json title="Example Payload"
{
  "event_id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000",
  "timestamp": "2024-07-04T14:48:00Z"
}
```

  "properties": {
    "event_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uuid",
      "description": "Unique identifier for the event"
    },
    "timestamp": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time",

schemaPath: schema.json

```json title="Example Payload"
  "timestamp": "2024-07-04T14:48:00Z"
}
```

    "timestamp": {
      "type": "string",

id: DeleteBudget
name: Delete Budget
version: 0.0.1
summary: Permanently remove a budget.
---

id: ImportTransactions
name: Import Transactions
version: 0.0.1
summary: Initiate a transaction import from Enable Banking.
---

---
id: RecordTransaction
name: Record Transaction
version: 0.0.1
summary: Record a new financial transaction.
---

id: TagTransaction
name: Tag Transaction
version: 0.0.1
summary: Apply tags to a transaction.
---

id: UpdateBudget
name: Update Budget
version: 0.0.1
summary: Modify an existing budget.
---

<NodeGraph />

---
id: UpdateTransactionNote
name: Update Transaction Note
version: 0.0.1
summary: Set or update a transaction's note.
---

## Overview

<NodeGraph />

---
id: CoreAggregate
name: Core Aggregate
version: 0.0.1
summary: Write-side aggregate handling finance commands and emitting events.
receives:
  - id: RecordTransaction
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: CategorizeTransaction
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: TagTransaction
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: UpdateTransactionNote
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: CreateBudget
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: UpdateBudget
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: DeleteBudget
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: ImportTransactions
    version: 0.0.1
sends:
  - id: TransactionRecorded
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: TransactionCategorized
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: TransactionTagged
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: TransactionNoteUpdated
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: BudgetCreated
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: BudgetUpdated
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: BudgetDeleted
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: BudgetReached
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: BudgetExceeded
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: ImportTransactionsRequested
    version: 0.0.1
writesTo:
  - id: CoreEventStore
    version: 0.0.1
---

## Overview

The Core Aggregate is the write-model of the Core domain. It receives commands from the client side and emit and append events into the [[container|CoreEventStore]].


> **Note:** [[event|TransactionImportCompleted]] and [[event|TransactionImportFailed]] are **not** emitted by this aggregate. They are written directly to the event store by the [[service|CoreEventInjector]] after the [[service|EnableBankingAPIGateway]] delivers the Enable Banking API response.

<NodeGraph />

---
id: CoreEventInjector
name: Core Event Injector
version: 0.0.1
summary: Writes transactions import outcome events to the event store.
sends:
  - id: TransactionImportCompleted
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: TransactionImportFailed
    version: 0.0.1
writesTo:
  - id: CoreEventStore
    version: 0.0.1
---

## Overview

The Core Event Injector writes events directly to the [[container|CoreEventStore]] without going through the [[service|CoreAggregate]] command pipeline. It is called directly by the [[service|EnableBankingAPIGateway]] after the Enable Banking API delivers its response.

On success it appends [[event|TransactionImportCompleted]] summarising the fetched transaction batch. On failure it appends [[event|TransactionImportFailed]] with the error context, allowing the projector and notifier to react accordingly.

<NodeGraph />

---
id: CoreNotifier
name: Core Notifier
version: 0.0.1
summary: Sends notifications to the clients/users
receives:
  - id: BudgetReached
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: BudgetExceeded
    version: 0.0.1
---

## Overview

The Core Notifier is a reactive service that subscribes to budget lifecycle events from the [[container|CoreEventStore]]. When a budget threshold is reached or exceeded, it dispatches user-facing notifications (e.g. push notifications or in-app alerts).

It does not emit any domain events back into the event store — notifications are fire-and-forget side effects.

```
BudgetReached   → [CoreNotifier] → BudgetThresholdReached notification (out-of-band)
BudgetExceeded  → [CoreNotifier] → BudgetExceeded notification (out-of-band)
```

<NodeGraph />

---
id: CoreProjector
name: Core Projector
version: 0.0.1
summary: Builds read models from the event store.
readsFrom:
  - id: CoreEventStore
    version: 0.0.1
writesTo:
  - id: TransactionView
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: BudgetView
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: DashboardView
    version: 0.0.1
---

## Overview

The Core Projector is the read-side component of the Core domain. It consumes all events from the [[container|CoreEventStore]] — written by both the [[service|CoreAggregate]] and the [[service|CoreEventInjector]] — and maintains three read models:

- **[[container|TransactionView]]** — per-transaction projection optimised for list and search queries.
- **[[container|BudgetView]]** — per-budget projection tracking spending progress and threshold status.
- **[[container|DashboardView]]** — aggregated financial summary for dashboard-style rendering.

<NodeGraph />

---
id: EnableBankingAPIGateway
name: Enable Banking API Gateway
version: 0.0.1
summary: Fetches transactions from Enable Banking and calls CoreEventInjector with the outcome.
receives:
  - id: ImportTransactionsRequested
    version: 0.0.1
flows:
  - id: TransactionImportFlow
    version: 0.0.1
---

## Overview

The Enable Banking API Gateway sits between the Core domain and the external [Enable Banking](https://enablebanking.com) open banking API. It has two responsibilities:

1. **Outbound** — subscribes to [[event|ImportTransactionsRequested]] event; on receipt, calls the Enable Banking API to fetch the user's transactions for the requested date range and account.
2. **Inbound** — once the Enable Banking API responds, calls the [[service|CoreEventInjector]] directly with the fetched transaction payload or the error details.


## External Systems

| System | Role |
|--------|------|
| **Enable Banking API** | External open banking aggregator. Provides access to user account transactions across connected banks. |

<NodeGraph />

---
id: Core
name: Core Domain
version: 0.0.1
summary: Manages personal finance transactions and budgets using event sourcing.
services:
  - id: CoreAggregate
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: CoreEventInjector
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: CoreNotifier
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: CoreProjector
    version: 0.0.1
  - id: EnableBankingAPIGateway
    version: 0.0.1
flows:
  - id: TransactionImportFlow
    version: 0.0.1
---

## Domain Overview

The Core domain is the foundational finance domain of Pennysheet. It manages the full lifecycle of financial transactions and budgets using an event-sourced architecture.


## Architecture

<NodeGraph />

---
id: BudgetView
name: Budget View
version: 0.0.1
summary: Per-budget read model tracking spending progress.
container_type: database
technology: postgres
classification: internal
access_mode: readWrite
schemaPath: schema.json
badges:
  - content: Read Model
    backgroundColor: purple
    textColor: white
---

## Overview

[[container|BudgetView]] is a database table (read model) maintained by the [[service|CoreProjector]]. It tracks the current spending state for each budget, including how much has been spent, whether the threshold has been reached, and whether the limit has been exceeded.

## Schema

<SchemaViewer file="schema.json" search="true" expand="true" />


 ## Raw Schema:schema.json

{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "id": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uuid",
      "description": "Unique identifier"
    },
    "placeholder_field": {
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Placeholder field"
    }
  },
  "required": ["id", "placeholder_field"]
}

---
id: CoreEventStore
name: Core Event Store
version: 0.0.1
summary: Append-only event table for all Core domain events.
container_type: database
technology: postgres
classification: internal
access_mode: appendOnly
---

## Overview

Core Event Store is the append-only event table for the Core domain. The events are emitted either [[service|CoreAggregate]] or [[service|CoreEventInjector]].

The [[service|CoreProjector]] and [[service|CoreNotifier]] consume events from this store to build read models and dispatch notifications.

---
id: DashboardView
name: Dashboard View
version: 0.0.1
summary: Aggregated financial summary for the dashboard.
container_type: database
technology: postgres
classification: internal
access_mode: readWrite
schemaPath: schema.json
badges:
  - content: Read Model
    backgroundColor: purple
    textColor: white
---

## Overview

[[container|DashboardView]] is a database table (read model) maintained by the [[service|CoreProjector]]. It provides a high-level aggregated summary of the user's financial picture — total income, total expenses, net balance, and budget health — optimised for dashboard-style rendering.

## Schema

<SchemaViewer file="schema.json" search="true" expand="true" />


 ## Raw Schema:schema.json

{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "id": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uuid",
      "description": "Unique identifier"
    },
    "placeholder_field": {
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Placeholder field"
    }
  },
  "required": ["id", "placeholder_field"]
}

---
id: TransactionView
name: Transaction View
version: 0.0.1
summary: Per-transaction read model for list and search.
container_type: database
technology: postgres
classification: internal
access_mode: readWrite
schemaPath: schema.json
badges:
  - content: Read Model
    backgroundColor: purple
    textColor: white
---

## Overview

[[container|TransactionView]] is a database table (read model) maintained by the [[service|CoreProjector]]. It provides a denormalised record of each transaction including its category, tags, note, and amount — optimised for paginated list rendering and full-text search.

## Schema

<SchemaViewer file="schema.json" search="true" expand="true" />


 ## Raw Schema:schema.json

{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "id": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uuid",
      "description": "Unique identifier"
    },
    "placeholder_field": {
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Placeholder field"
    }
  },
  "required": ["id", "placeholder_field"]
}

---
id: TransactionImportFlow
name: Transaction Import Flow
version: 0.0.1
summary: Import transactions from Enable Banking — request to completion or failure.
steps:

  - id: user
    title: User
    actor:
      name: User
    next_step:
      id: import_cmd
      label: via client-side application

  - id: import_cmd
    title: Import Transactions
    message:
      id: ImportTransactions
      version: 0.0.1
    next_step:
      id: core_aggregate
      label: handled by aggregate

  - id: core_aggregate
    title: Core Aggregate
    service:
      id: CoreAggregate
      version: 0.0.1
    next_step:
      id: import_requested
      label: emits events

  - id: import_requested
    title: Import Transactions Requested
    message:
      id: ImportTransactionsRequested
      version: 0.0.1
    next_step:
      id: enable_banking_gateway
      label: received by API Gateway

  - id: enable_banking_gateway
    title: Enable Banking API Gateway
    service:
      id: EnableBankingAPIGateway
      version: 0.0.1
    next_step:
      id: enable_banking_api
      label: fetches transactions

  - id: enable_banking_api
    title: Enable Banking API
    externalSystem:
      name: Enable Banking API
      summary: Open banking aggregator that provides access to user account transactions across connected banks.
      url: https://enablebanking.com
    next_step:
      id: enable_banking_gateway_response
      label: returns transactions or error response

  - id: enable_banking_gateway_response
    title: Enable Banking API Gateway (response received)
    service:
      id: EnableBankingAPIGateway
      version: 0.0.1
    next_step:
      id: core_event_injector
      label: call event injector

  - id: core_event_injector
    title: Core Event Injector
    service:
      id: CoreEventInjector
      version: 0.0.1
    next_steps:
      - id: import_completed
        label: Import succeeded
      - id: import_failed
        label: Import failed

  - id: import_completed
    title: Transaction Import Completed
    message:
      id: TransactionImportCompleted
      version: 0.0.1

  - id: import_failed
    title: Transaction Import Failed
    message:
      id: TransactionImportFailed
      version: 0.0.1

---

End-to-end import flow for the Core domain. When a user requests a transaction import, this flow drives the fetch from the Enable Banking open banking API and asynchronously records the outcome in the event store.

<NodeGraph />

---
dictionary:
  - id: Aggregate
    name: Aggregate
    summary: "Write-side component that handles commands and emits events for the Transaction and Budget aggregate roots."
    description: |
      The CoreAggregate is the consistency boundary for all finance operations. All successful state transitions are appended as events to the CoreEventStore.
    icon: Box

  - id: Transaction
    name: Transaction
    summary: "A single financial movement — income or expense — recorded by the user."
    description: |
      Identified by a transaction_id. Lifecycle events: TransactionRecorded, TransactionCategorized, TransactionTagged, TransactionNoteUpdated. Transactions are immutable once recorded; enrichment (categorisation, tagging, notes) is modelled as separate events.
    icon: ArrowLeftRight

  - id: Budget
    name: Budget
    summary: "A user-defined spending limit for a category over a time period."
    description: |
      Lifecycle: BudgetCreated → (BudgetUpdated) → BudgetReached → BudgetExceeded → (BudgetDeleted). Budgets track cumulative spending and trigger threshold notifications when limits approach or are breached.
    icon: Wallet

  - id: EventStore
    name: Event Store
    summary: "Append-only log that persists all domain events — the source of truth for the Core domain."
    description: |
      The CoreEventStore holds every event emitted by the CoreAggregate and injected by the CoreEventInjector. The CoreProjector and CoreNotifier consume from this store to build read models and dispatch notifications.
    icon: Database

  - id: EventInjector
    name: Event Injector
    summary: "Service that writes events directly to the event store, bypassing the command pipeline — used for transaction imports."
    description: |
      The CoreEventInjector handles scenarios where events originate outside the normal command flow, such as CSV imports from a bank export. It appends a TransactionImportCompleted event when the import finishes.
    icon: ArrowDownToLine

  - id: Projector
    name: Projector
    summary: "Read-side component that consumes events and maintains denormalised query-optimised views."
    description: |
      The CoreProjector listens to all Core events and keeps three read models up to date: TransactionView (per-transaction list), BudgetView (spending progress), and DashboardView (aggregated financial summary).
    icon: LayoutDashboard

  - id: Notifier
    name: Notifier
    summary: "Reactive service that dispatches user notifications when budget thresholds are reached or exceeded."
    description: |
      The CoreNotifier subscribes to BudgetReached and BudgetExceeded events and sends out-of-band notifications (e.g. push notifications or in-app alerts). It does not write back to the event store.
    icon: Bell

  - id: TransactionView
    name: Transaction View
    summary: "Read model table providing a per-transaction projection for list and search queries."
    description: |
      Maintained by the CoreProjector. Contains denormalised transaction data including category, tags, note, amount, and date.
    icon: List

  - id: BudgetView
    name: Budget View
    summary: "Read model table tracking spending progress and threshold status per budget."
    description: |
      Maintained by the CoreProjector. Tracks how much has been spent against each budget, whether the threshold has been reached, and whether the limit has been exceeded.
    icon: BarChart

  - id: DashboardView
    name: Dashboard View
    summary: "Read model table providing an aggregated financial summary for dashboard rendering."
    description: |
      Maintained by the CoreProjector. Summarises total income, total expenses, net balance, and overall budget health for a user.
    icon: LayoutGrid

  - id: TransactionImport
    name: Transaction Import
    summary: "The process of ingesting transactions from an external source such as a bank CSV export via Enable Banking."
    description: |
      Triggered by the ImportTransactions command. The CoreEventInjector writes TransactionImportCompleted or TransactionImportFailed to the CoreEventStore after the Enable Banking API responds.
    icon: Upload
---
